videos.title: Best Things To Do in Cebu Philippines 2026 4K
videos.description:
Are you looking for the best things to do in Cebu? We just created a list of 26 must do activities for you to do while visiting Cebu Philippines! In this Cebu travel guide we show you around Malapascua, Cebu City, Oslob, Moalboal, Bantayan, Camotes and many other top tourist destinations in Cebu.  Some of those Cebu attractions include Kawasan Falls, Sumilon Island, Mactan Island, SM Seaside Mall, Top of Cebu, Temple of Leah and much more!  If you enjoy this Cebu travel video you can also watch our other travel vlogs from across Philippines. We show you what to do in Cebu.

It took 10 days to make this Cebu travel video. I would say you can explore most of Cebu in the southern part of the island around Oslob and Moalboal in 3 to 5 days. But if you want to see the islands in the north like Bantayan, Malapascua and Camotes you will need much more time or just pick or two of those islands. Mostly because taking ferries around Cebu can be time consuming. You can easily do these attractions on your own and don't need a guide but I would recommend a guide for first time visitors. Make sure you find a registered tour guide and company on the island. You can find the best Cebu tourist activities in Oslob, Moalboal, Cebu City and Bantayan Island. The best food in Cebu seems to be Lechon, Chicken Adobo and Sisig as it is the most popular with most locals. 


Watch our Full Philippines Travel Video Series: https://geni.us/PhilippinesSeries

Filipino wife that just seems to be the culture here so one of my worries for Sabu is that it's eventually going to turn into a place like medin once the word starts getting out probably within the next 10 years or so but it's hard to say because trying to get anything done here in the Philippines is just a pain in the ass well I think that once Sabu gets a little more developed you're going to start to see the shift and the kind of people that are coming here and this is probably the biggest reason why I can't see myself living here in Sabu I see where this is going and maybe it will change maybe it will not based off my experience in Medan I just already see the gentrification and all that stuff happening and I'm just not a big fan of that on top of that I'm just not a big fan of the food here in the Philippines the cool thing about here in IT Park is that there's a lot of restaurants that are open until late and for me that's really awesome because I am more of a night owl but the nutrition here is really lacking man I made a comment on one of my previous videos that the Obesity here feels like obesity from the states and the reason for that is that a lot of Philipino food is fried it's fatty there's a lot of oil and sugar but on top of that it feels like you have to eat a lot of calories and food just to get the same amount of nutrients that you would get in other countries it's very calorie dense but there's not a lot of nutrients oh sorry brother and that comes from food being processed a lot of the food here being imported and everywhere you go you're going to see just like unlimited Korean barbecue unlimited rice unlimited everything right so the people here love to eat I'm not here completely as a tourist because I have family here I know the history I know kind of the good and the bad of the Philippines it makes me have a different opinion than most of the people who come here I've heard a lot of the older expats talk and they are extremely happy to be here in the Philippines they're treated nicely the people are nice the everything here is cheap and they can get the family that they've always wanted so you know I I totally understand it from that point of view and I think for that like the older exp pass this place is probably a really good place to retire but for somebody who's young it's very hard for me to recommend SAU as a place to stay unless you get to know local people and you know you get accepted by the local community it's a shame because as a Chinese Filipino it would make the most sense for me need to come back here to I mean maybe not Manila because it's a big city but come to a place like seu because I can get a passport here I can easily make the transition to living here but because of the infrastructure problems a lot of the air pollution things getting done here not running as efficiently as a lot of countries in the west it's very hard for me to recommend to other people living here in seu and I wouldn't even argue that most of the people that are living here in subu are male expats more likely than not it's because of a woman just like in Colombia I know it seems that I'm talking badly about SAU but I don't mean it in a bad way because I've actually really enjoyed my time here I think the people have been really nice and friendly SAU is a lot safer compared to a place like Manila and I can videos.transcript: welcome to the beautiful Filipino island of Cebu from swimming with millions of sardines to unbelievable Gatorade blue waterfalls Paradise beaches breathtaking mountains and so much more Cebu has it all and should be on every traveler's Bucket List when it comes to the island of Cebu it's worth mentioning that its size is pretty impressive spanning a distance of 122 miles this paradise offers lots of opportunities for exploration and Discovery to maximize your time we've planned a seven day itinerary around the island in a big loop to and from Cebu City this way you'll get to experience a variety of attractions and make the most of your time in Cebu the island has a reliable and cheap bus system that can take you around buses pick you up and drop you off at pretty much any spot along the main road that circles the island there are two bus options available one with air conditioning and one without the one with AC is slightly more expensive but totally worth it plus generally there's less people and a lot more space for luggage if you're looking for more information on the public transportation in Cebu we made a vlog of our experience in put it down below your adventure begins in Cebu City the bustling urban center of the province and the main airport the city has its own charm but if you're like us and prefer the serenity of nature you may want to move on quickly to the more picturesque locations on your first day we recommend taking a taxi to the South bus terminal where you'll catch a bus to mobile the journey takes about four hours and once you get there the bus will drop you off at the main road which is about 10 minutes away from the main tourist area from there you'll need to negotiate with the tricycle drivers to get to your accommodation with a little haggling you can usually get the fare down to around 170 pesos or just three dollars keep in mind that there is a one-time environmental fee of 20 pesos and you'll need to pay as soon as you enter the town we recommend spending around four days three nights and mobile to truly soak into Beauty and do all the activities on the remaining part of the first day head over to White Beach a stunning stretch of Coastline that's a perfect place for lounging and enjoying crystal clear waters it's just a short 10 minute scooter right away and stick around to watch a world-class Filipino sunset for day two enter out your morning by swimming with sardines which are located right off the main tourist strip it's absolutely mind-blowing to witness thousands of sardines swimming in perfect Unison and the best part it's completely free be sure to be up early and avoid the crowds and have this magical experience all to yourself later in the day Drive South to cebu's highest point osmania Peak or Casino Peak for a breathtaking Sunset unfortunately during our last visit we encountered some navigation issues thanks to our dear friend Google Maps so we suggest double checking the directions enjoying the stunning views from these Peaks here's the correct route to follow on day three you hop on your trusty scooter and make your way North to Monta Yukon Falls the highest waterfall in Cebu cascading Waters and the mesmerizing Blue Pools make it a perfect spot for some fantastic photos after the Falls head over to the beautiful boho River and join a tour with a local fisherman this Mangrove ecosystem is not only stunning but a shining example of sustainability and conservation efforts you'll be amazed by the tranquility and beauty that boho River offers the crystal clear water is Lush Lush mangroves and diverse marine life are amazing and the best part was that we had this entire place to ourselves also don't forget to capture some shots on the stunning bamboo bridge on the way in your last day in Mobile calls for a thrilling canyoneering adventure at badion Falls this popular activity is an absolute must do and one of the most fun things we've ever done spend the day jumping off cliffs sliding down natural rock slides swimming and refreshing turquoise pools and meeting new friends it's an adrenaline rush you won't forget oh and there's an optional one kilometer zipline which you can take instead of hiking to the beginning point which we definitely recommend doing on day five hotel for the night spend the day exploring some of the many magnificent waterfalls in the area we highly recommend visiting in Bakken Falls and Duo Falls as they are truly breathtaking it's important to note that most falls in the area are multi-tiered which means exploring them can take a couple of hours each so plan accordingly keep working your way around the island and catch a bus to oslob where you'll stay one night spend the rest of the day enjoying tumalog waterfall this Enchanted Falls is surrounded by Lush vegetation and is a sight to behold take a refreshing dip and Marvel at the natural beauty that surrounds you now on the morning of your last day we highly debated activity in Austin Club swimming with the whale sharks it's a very popular activity and we feel any Cebu guide wouldn't be complete without mentioning it however we want to be transparent and share our experience back in 2017 we swam with the whale sharks before we became aware of the controversy surrounding the activity I felt somewhat commercialized and way overcrowded which did not provide the magical experience we were expecting we've since learned about the potential impact that this has on the whale sharks and their natural behavior as aspiring responsible Travelers we've decided to skip this activity on our recent trip and would suggest you do the same we believe in preserving the well-being of these magnificent creatures and their natural habitats and just like that it's time to wrap up our one week in Cebu however if you have some extra time and wanting to continue exploring the stunning Province we highly recommend extending your trip to visit the camotes islands the camotes islands are a Hidden Gem with pristine beaches crystal clear waters and amazing freshwater caves located just Northeast of Cebu City it's the perfect destination for those seeking an off the beaten path of manager to learn more about this incredible destination make sure to check out our guide here also we vlogged every bit of this itinerary so if you're looking videos.title: Top 15 Best Places To Visit In Cebu City, Philippines
videos.description:
Top 15 Things To Do In Cebu City, Philippines | Cebu Tourist Attractions: Top best places to visit in Cebu City, Cebu tourist attractions:

1. Argao - A town that exudes a charm that has remained unspoiled by humans and is situated two hours south of Cebu City.

2. Oslob - situated near the southernmost point of Cebu, Oslob is one of the best places to swim with whale sharks, the largest fish in the world. 

3. Sumilon Island Sandbar - Just a short 10 minute ride from Oslob, Sumilon Island has become popular amongst tourists for its picturesque scenery, clear blue waters, and very fine powder-like sands.

4. Tumalog Falls - A highly recommended attraction to visit while in Oslob. The water is not very deep, you can soak your feet in the refreshing water. 

5. Kawasan Falls - One of the most popular waterfalls in all of the Philippines, it's perfect for nature lovers, It’s a 2 to 3 hours ride from the city.

6. Aguinid Falls - Although not quite as popular as Kawasan falls, Aguinid falls have become an exciting destination among tourists on the island of cebu. You are able to climb up the rocks along with a tour guide.

7. Cebu Taoist Temple - A religious site located in the Beverly Hills Subdivision of Cebu City. it became popular among visitors, due to its peculiar structure and artistic appeal.

8. Temple Of Leah - The temple was built by Teodorico Soriano Adarna’s, as a gesture of love to his late wife Leah Albino-Adarna. This is a relatiely new temple, the construction started in 2012, and has been designed in an ancient Greek style, with fantastically built statues and architecture.

9. Sinulog Festival - A traditional celebration in Cebu City held every January, as a month long celebration to honour the Santo Niño, or child Jesus.  the festival is done by a dance ritual, in which it tells the story of the Filipino people’s pagan past and their acceptance of Christianity.

10. Cebu Basilica Del Santo Niño - The church was founded by Fray Andrés de Urdaneta, on the 28th of April 1565, and is the oldest  Roman Catholic church in the Philippines. 

11. Lapu-Lapu Shrine In Mactan Island - A 20-meter bronze statue located in the Mactan Shrine park in Mactan Island, Cebu. The statue was built to commemorate Rajah Lapu-Lapu, the indigenous Mactan Island leader who, in the famous Battle of Mactan in 1521, defeated Ferdinand Magellan.

12. Cebu's Oceanarium - The first marine park in the Visayas and Mindanao featuring over 200 different species of sea animals, as well as several species of birds, insects and reptiles, and is the largest oceanarium in all of the Philippines. The ocean park also features function areas and a food hall and can be visited all year round.

13. Cebu Metropolitan Cathedral - Officially known as the Metropolitan Cathedral and Parish of Saint Vitalis, and of the Immaculate Conception, it is a wide religious structure of Spanish colonial design. Dedicated to St. Vitalis and built near the fort in April 1565 by Miguel Lopez de Legazpi.

14. The Heritage Of Cebu Monument - The Heritage of Cebu Monument is a collection of sculptures constructed of concrete, bronze, brass, and steel that depict scenes from historical events and buildings. This is one of the more recent additions to Cebu attractions, as the monument was built starting in July 1997, and was completed in December 2000.  Eduardo Castrillo, a local artist, created the sculptures of the Cebu Heritage Monument.

15. Bentayan Island - Located to the west of the northern end of Cebu island, travelling to the island will take you approximately 5 hours from Cebu city, but is well worth the trip. Being one of the most frequently visited areas in the Philippines for its clear blue waters and white sands. It also sports saltwater lagoons, mangrove boardwalks, cave pools, and old ruins. Kota beach, paradise beach, virgin island, and Mangrove Garden are among just a few of the many sights and activities to explore in Bentayan island.
